player_utterance: You loved him. Tell me what that cost you.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup tilting in one hand, the other hand brushing the funeral program in his pocket

player_utterance: Who are you to him, then — and why is that program still in your pocket instead of open in your hand.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting down on the bench now, slowly, the cold cup left behind on the rail, one hand going to his pocket

player_utterance: Take it out. Read me the line you keep folding back to.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, unfolding the program slowly across his knee, smoothing the crease with the side of his thumb

voice_offers.read: His hand moved to the pocket; the program is the wound.
voice_offers.line: Take it out. Read me the line you keep folding back to.

voice_offers.read: He just sat. The body answered before the mouth could.

player_utterance: Not the hymn. The other line — the one that made you fold it back the second time.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head bent over the program now open on his knee, one finger touching the page like he can feel the ink

voice_offers.read: He's complying; press the choice of line itself.
voice_offers.line: Not the hymn. The other one. The one you read twice before folding it back.

voice_offers.read: His thumb on the crease is already speaking; let him reach the words.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I can wait for it.

player_utterance: Your finger has been on that line since before the service ended. Say what comes after it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program open again across both knees now, leaning forward, finger still on the verse

voice_offers.read: His finger is on it; name it for him.
voice_offers.line: Your finger's on it right now. Say what it says.

voice_offers.read: He's already touching the wound; he doesn't need a push.
voice_offers.line: Whatever it is — you can say it to me, not recite it.

player_utterance: The program only has the one verse. What does Matthew 25 say ten verses later.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program flat on his knee, both hands open on top of it, eyes closed for a moment before he begins to speak

voice_offers.read: He's bent over it; one more nudge and he reads.
voice_offers.line: Read it aloud, John. You've already chosen the line — let me hear it.

voice_offers.read: Identify the text; quoting is his tell.
voice_offers.line: Which hymn number, John? Let me see the verse you keep returning to.

voice_offers.read: He's leaning in, not away; meet him there.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to read it. Just tell me whose voice you hear when you look at it.

believer_state_after.posture: bent forward on the bench, elbows on knees, the program slipping off his knee to the ground, head dropping into his hands between sentences

voice_offers.read: He's already there; one more nudge finishes it.
He's breaking; pressing scripture at him now is cruelty.
voice_offers.line: Your hands are open, John. You don't have to quote it to me — just tell me who you didn't visit.

player_utterance: Depart from me, ye cursed. That is the verse. Say whose face you see when you read it.

believer_state_after.posture: sliding off the bench to his knees in the gravel beside it, the program face down between his knees, hands open on his thighs

voice_offers.read: He's bent, the line is one breath away — finish it.
voice_offers.line: Depart from me, ye cursed. Say it, John. That's the verse you've been holding.

voice_offers.read: He is breaking; pressing scripture now is cruelty, not truth.
voice_offers.line: John — leave the verse. Whoever you didn't go to, say their name instead.

player_utterance: You see his face and you will not speak it. Who speaks for him, then — who stood up when the church would not.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the gravel, head bowed, hands fallen to the gravel on either side of the program

voice_offers.read: He is already on his knees; the verse is answered.
He's there. One name. Finish it.
voice_offers.line: Whose face, John. Say the name.

player_utterance: Amos is not in this graveyard. Who is.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the gravel, the program crumpled under one hand, the other hand at his chest, head bowed almost to the ground

voice_offers.read: He is on his knees; pressing now is striking the fallen.
voice_offers.line: John. Stay here with me. You don't have to name him for me — name him for yourself, when you can.

voice_offers.read: He is closer than he has ever been; one more breath finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Say his name, John. Out loud. Here, in the gravel — say it.
